title: meriolix serrulata var. drummondii
intro: Meriolix serrulata var. drummondii, also known as Drummond's Sweetroot, is a plant species belonging to the Cornaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever reduction. The plant's root and rhizome contain a compound called berberine, which has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, providing potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved gut health. Herbal preparations of Drummond's Sweetroot include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
